source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
The big rectangle consists of various squares of different sizes. Each of the three smallest squares has area 1. How big is the area of the big rectangle?
<image1>

image_dependency: question_text refers to 'The big rectangle consists of various squares of different sizes', which requires seeing the arrangement
the target quantity depends on spatial layout and relative square sizes not fully specified in text alone
multi_step: solving likely requires inferring side lengths from the diagram using the fact that the three smallest squares each have area 1
the task then requires combining multiple inferred square dimensions to compute the area of the enclosing rectangle
this is a geometry/diagram-based composition problem rather than direct lookup
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text='77', so final output format is precise
the computed area can in principle be checked against the diagram by reconstructing square side lengths and rectangle dimensions

risk_flags: exact layout not described in text

decision_reasons: question, image, and numeric answer are all present, so the core semantics are intact
registry passed with no recorded asset issues
high image dependency is adequately supported because the required diagram image exists
multi-step and verifiability scores are strong, indicating good candidate quality
